title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and interview transcripts into a PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine built on sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline spanning OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama models to generate explainable skill gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that strictly grounded all outputs in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the complete system through an interactive Textual terminal UI, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices enabling auditable, evidence-backed hiring decisions.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM, trained on housing market data with features such as location, square footage, and property characteristics. Developed an interactive Streamlit dashboard with dynamic search and Plotly visualizations to deliver real-time price estimates to end users. The pipeline leveraged scikit-learn for preprocessing and model evaluation, with joblib-serialized models enabling efficient inference without retraining on each request.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to optimize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving ~90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, revealing conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: Built a data science project using Python to retrieve, process, and visualize structured datasets through API requests and pandas-based data manipulation. Applied statistical analysis and created comprehensive visualizations using matplotlib and seaborn to uncover patterns and trends within the data. Demonstrated end-to-end data science workflow from raw data acquisition to insight generation within a Jupyter Notebook environment.

description: Unable to generate a meaningful description without access to the repository's actual content, code, or findings. Please provide details such as the dataset used, methods applied, and key results so an accurate and specific portfolio description can be written.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, notebook-based pipeline to identify "sleeper cell" candidates in fantasy sports using Python, pandas, and the Requests library to fetch and process player data from external APIs. Applied custom filtering and scoring logic to surface undervalued players by comparing performance metrics against roster ownership rates. The tool provided actionable draft and waiver wire recommendations by surfacing high-upside players likely overlooked in standard fantasy leagues.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack fantasy football roster optimization tool that connects to the Sleeper API to surface a user's leagues, rosters, and available player data in a React + Vite frontend. Leveraged Python with pandas and NumPy in a Jupyter Notebook backend to process player statistics and power an AI-driven roster-building assistant. The system translates raw API data into actionable lineup recommendations, helping users make informed roster decisions within their Sleeper fantasy leagues.

title: DSC 80 Project Notebook
description: Built a data science pipeline using pandas, scikit-learn, and Plotly to clean, transform, and model a structured dataset, applying techniques such as feature engineering, imputation, and supervised learning. Developed interactive visualizations with Plotly and matplotlib to communicate patterns and model performance across key variables. Evaluated multiple sklearn models to identify the best-performing approach based on quantitative metrics.

title: UCSD Research Lab
description: Built a research project for UCSD supporting the work of Andre Gray, leveraging Jupyter Notebooks and R to conduct data-driven investigations across statistical modeling and computational workflows. Implemented reproducible analysis pipelines combining Python and R-based methodologies to process and interpret research datasets. The project delivered structured, documented findings to support ongoing academic research initiatives at UC San Diego.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive visualization of bicycle traffic patterns across Cambridge using JavaScript, CSS, and HTML. Mapped bike lane usage and traffic volume data onto a dynamic interface, allowing users to filter and examine ridership trends across different streets and time periods. The project translated raw municipal traffic datasets into an accessible, browser-based tool for understanding urban cycling infrastructure.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This tutorial project walked through the foundational workflow of GitHub Desktop, demonstrating how to initialize a repository, edit files locally, and sync changes with a remote repository. The exercise used a README file as a hands-on introduction to version control concepts, including staging, committing, and pushing updates. The project established a baseline understanding of Git-based collaboration tools essential for managing code in professional development environments.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
